meaning: vi.拖足而行,磨损vt.以足擦地,践踏,使磨损n.拖着脚走,磨损之处
examples: Constant wheelchair use will scuff almost any floor surface.
Scuff marks from shoes are difficult to remove.
If you scuff a shoe, he reveals, "a Sharpie the color of your shoe can save the day!"
They also scuff up quickly and have to be mandatorily replaced after 35 overs in one-day matches.
After Julian had gone to bed one night, he heard the back door to the main hall scuff open.
